questions.question: What is the time complexity of `list.pop(0)` and what is the O(1) alternative?
questions.answer: O(n) — shifts all elements. Use `collections.deque.popleft()` for O(1).

questions.question: Why is string concatenation with `+=` in a loop O(n²) in Python?
questions.answer: Strings are immutable; each `+=` creates a new copy of the entire string. Total characters copied = 1+2+...+n = O(n²). Use `''.join(list)` instead.

questions.question: What bug does `[10, 9, 2, 1, 100].sort()` introduce in JavaScript?
questions.answer: Sorts lexicographically (string order) giving [1, 10, 100, 2, 9]. Fix: `.sort((a, b) => a - b)`.

questions.question: In Java binary search, what causes the midpoint calculation `(lo + hi) / 2` to be unsafe?
questions.answer: Integer overflow when lo + hi > Integer.MAX_VALUE. Use `lo + (hi - lo) / 2` instead.
